summary: Matched Link's Side-B boomerang special by moving a large block out of `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftLk_Init.s` into new C sources, primarily `ftLk_SpecialS.c`, with supporting Link Special-N extraction and header/type cleanup. The PR renamed several previously address-based helpers into boomerang-oriented functions, added the new objects to the build, exposed item/fighter prototypes needed by the matched C, and clarified Link fighter vars/dat attributes used by boomerang state and aiming.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftLink/ftLk_SpecialS.c` with 460 lines implementing Link Side-B behavior: entry functions, boomerang removal helpers, angle/position calculation, accessory callback spawning/updating the boomerang item, anim/IASA/phys/collision handlers, and ground/air transition behavior.
Reduced `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftLk_Init.s` by over 1100 lines, leaving the matched Side-B/Special-N-related code in C and only retaining asm references that still needed symbol-name updates.

Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftLink/ftLk_SpecialN.c` with supporting Special-N helper functions such as `ftLk_SpecialN_GetIndex`, `ftLk_SpecialN_UnsetFv10`, `ftLk_SpecialN_UnsetFv14`, `ftLk_SpecialN_ProcessFv10`, and `ftLk_SpecialN_ProcessFv14`.
Renamed `ftLk_800EAD64` to the more descriptive `ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists` and changed call sites in asm and C to use that symbol.

Renamed Side-B item callback helpers from address-style names to intent-bearing names including `ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0`, `ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang1`, `ftLk_Is2071b0_5to13`, `ftLk_Is2071b0_1to13`, and `ftLk_SepcialS_Get2219b5`.
Updated item asm references in `asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s` so item-side boomerang logic calls the newly named Link Side-B helper symbols.

Expanded `ftLink/types.h` so Link dat attributes at offsets `x14`, `x18`, `x1C`, `x20`, and `x24` are typed as floats for Side-B aiming/distance logic, and renamed Link fighter vars from raw `x0`/`x8` fields to `used_boomerang` and `boomerang_gobj`.
Updated CLink init code to use the same renamed fighter var concepts (`used_boomerang`, `boomerang_gobj`) when clearing boomerang state on death.
Added `ftLk_SpecialNIndex` enum to `forward.h` for indexing Link's neutral special states from motion IDs.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to build `ftLk_SpecialS.c.o` and `ftLk_SpecialN.c.o` before the remaining Link init asm object.
smart_moves: The match split the extracted logic by move domain: Side-B behavior lives in `ftLk_SpecialS.c`, while adjacent projectile cleanup/state helpers for Special-N were separated into `ftLk_SpecialN.c` instead of leaving everything in init code.
The PR converted opaque fighter-var offsets into semantic boomerang fields, making future item/fighter interactions easier to reason about while preserving offset comments in the struct.
It used descriptive callback names tied to fighter callback slots, such as `on21EC` and `onAccessory4`, to keep evidence of original callback placement while clarifying behavior.

The C implementation preserved matching-sensitive details with explicit `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused-stack padding in helper functions such as `calcAnglePos`, `onAccessory4`, and Special-N cleanup routines.
The boomerang angle calculation was expressed in high-level math (`atan2f`, `cosf`, `sinf`, `M_PI`) while still matching facing-direction wrapping behavior from the assembly.
The build change placed the new C object files before the remaining `ftLk_Init.s.o`, consistent with replacing only part of the prior asm range while preserving link order.
Renaming item-callable helper symbols in the asm files avoided leaving stale `ftLk_800EC03C`/`ftLk_800EBFDC` references after extracting the implementations to C.
decomp_lessons: When extracting a large fighter init asm blob, first identify move-specific regions and migrate them to move-specific C files rather than leaving everything under `Init`.

For fighter vars, rename fields only when call-site evidence is strong. Here `used_boomerang` and `boomerang_gobj` are supported by repeated checks, item creation via `it_802A013C`, item removal via `it_802A07B4`, and death cleanup.

Projectile specials often need coordinated fighter and item changes: the fighter C implementation, item asm references, item headers, and death/damage callbacks all need to agree on helper names and prototypes.

Preserve callback-slot information during decompilation. Names like `x21EC_callback`, `x21BC_callback_Accessory4`, `x21E8_callback_OnDeath3`, and `x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage` explain why small helper functions are called indirectly.
Enums derived from contiguous motion IDs can simplify state indexing; this PR added `ftLk_SpecialNIndex` and computed it as `msid - ftLk_MS_SpecialNStart` for Link neutral-special states.
Adding concrete struct fields to dat attributes can unlock clean C for math-heavy functions, but offset comments remain important because adjacent unknown fields may still be placeholders.
For matching C that manipulates animation command variables, a local enum such as `cmd_var_idx` is useful documentation without requiring global names for every command variable yet.
naming_conventions: Move files follow the fighter/move pattern `ftLk_SpecialS.c`, `ftLk_SpecialS.h`, `ftLk_SpecialN.c`, and `ftLk_SpecialN.h`.
Extracted boomerang-state helpers use action-oriented names: `ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang0`, `ftLk_SpecialS_RemoveBoomerang1`, and `ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists`.
Boolean range-check helpers were named directly from known bitfield ranges when semantic names were not yet known: `ftLk_Is2071b0_5to13` and `ftLk_Is2071b0_1to13`.
The PR retains an apparent typo in the exported symbol `ftLk_SepcialS_Get2219b5`; future work should preserve it unless deliberately renaming with all call sites updated.

Link-specific fighter vars in `struct ftLink_FighterVars` now prefer semantic names where known (`used_boomerang`, `boomerang_gobj`) and `xNN` placeholders where purpose remains weak (`x10`, `x14`, `x18`).
Motion-state names use existing Link conventions such as `ftLk_MS_SpecialS1`, `ftLk_MS_SpecialS1Empty`, `ftLk_MS_SpecialAirS1`, and `ftLk_MS_SpecialAirS1Empty`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` dummy stack arrays to preserve otherwise unused stack space in functions where the decompiled C did not naturally allocate the same stack frame.
Used a local static helper `calcAnglePos` to match the assembly pattern for clamping vertical stick angle, computing a position vector, and normalizing the returned angle based on facing direction.
Used `fabs_inline` rather than a library call in stick-threshold checks to mirror the original absolute-value codegen.

Kept small wrappers and duplicated entry/collision code when inlining or sharing would not match; the source explicitly notes that a simple inline would not work for sharing `ftLk_SpecialS_Enter` and `ftLk_SpecialAirS_Enter`.
Maintained object-file/link ordering by adding C objects before the residual `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftLk_Init.s.o` in `obj_files.mk`.
Updated branch labels/symbol references inside remaining asm files to call the newly matched C symbols, avoiding duplicate or missing symbol definitions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only available human context is the title, changed files, and diff; there is no explicit reviewer rationale to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftLk_SepcialS_Get2219b5` or give semantic names to `ftLk_Is2071b0_5to13` and `ftLk_Is2071b0_1to13`.
Search for future cleanup of `ftLink_FighterVars` fields `x10`, `x14`, and `x18` after this boomerang/Special-N extraction.
Look for later PRs that replace `on21EC`, `onAccessory4`, or `cmd_unk0_bool` with more descriptive names.
Find subsequent work on `it_27CF` boomerang item C matching that consumes the Link helper prototypes added here.
Search for PRs that remove the `MUST_MATCH` unused stack padding in Link Side-B or Special-N helpers.
